concepts.label: World War II
concepts.definition: Global military conflict from 1939 to 1945 involving most world nations, primarily between the Axis and Allied powers

concepts.label: World War I
concepts.definition: Global conflict from 1914 to 1918 involving many of the world's great powers, primarily centered in Europe

concepts.label: Democracy
concepts.definition: System of government where power is vested in the people through elected representatives

concepts.label: Monarchy
concepts.definition: Form of government where a single person, usually called a monarch, rules as head of state for life

concepts.label: Republic
concepts.definition: Form of government where power resides in elected officials representing the citizen body

concepts.label: Fascism
concepts.definition: Authoritarian ultranationalist political ideology characterized by dictatorial power and forcible suppression of opposition

concepts.label: Communism
concepts.definition: Political and economic ideology advocating for a classless society where all property and wealth are communally owned

concepts.label: Socialism
concepts.definition: Political and economic theory advocating collective or governmental ownership and administration of means of production

concepts.label: Capitalism
concepts.definition: Economic system based on private ownership of means of production and creation of goods for profit

concepts.label: Adolf Hitler
concepts.definition: German politician and leader of Nazi Germany from 1934 to 1945, responsible for the Holocaust and World War II

concepts.label: Winston Churchill
concepts.definition: British Prime Minister during World War II, known for his leadership during Britain's fight against Nazi Germany

concepts.label: Franklin D. Roosevelt
concepts.definition: 32nd President of the United States who led the country during the Great Depression and World War II

concepts.label: Joseph Stalin
concepts.definition: Soviet dictator who led the USSR from the mid-1920s until his death in 1953

concepts.label: Napoleon Bonaparte
concepts.definition: French military leader and emperor who conquered much of continental Europe in the early 19th century

concepts.label: Julius Caesar
concepts.definition: Roman general and statesman who played a critical role in the events that led to the demise of the Roman Republic

concepts.label: Augustus
concepts.definition: First Roman Emperor, originally named Gaius Octavius, who transformed Rome from republic to empire

concepts.label: Charlemagne
concepts.definition: King of the Franks and Lombards who was crowned Emperor of the Romans by Pope Leo III in 800 CE

concepts.label: Otto Von Bismarck
concepts.definition: Prussian statesman who dominated German and European affairs from the 1860s until 1890, unified Germany

concepts.label: Martin Luther
concepts.definition: German theologian and reformer whose actions started the Protestant Reformation in the early 16th century

concepts.label: Mahatma Gandhi
concepts.definition: Indian independence activist who employed nonviolent resistance to lead the successful campaign for India's independence

concepts.label: Alexander The Great
concepts.definition: King of ancient Greek kingdom of Macedon who created one of the largest empires in history

concepts.label: Cleopatra VII
concepts.definition: Last active pharaoh of Ptolemaic Egypt who maintained power through political alliance with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony

concepts.label: Roman Empire
concepts.definition: Ancient empire centered on Rome that dominated the Mediterranean region from 27 BCE to 476/1453 CE

concepts.label: Byzantine Empire
concepts.definition: Eastern continuation of the Roman Empire lasting from approximately 330 to 1453 CE

concepts.label: Holy Roman Empire
concepts.definition: Multi-ethnic political entity in Western, Central and Southern Europe that lasted from 962 to 1806

concepts.label: Ottoman Empire
concepts.definition: Turkish empire that controlled much of Southeast Europe, Western Asia and North Africa from 14th to early 20th century

concepts.label: British Empire
concepts.definition: Global empire comprising dominions, colonies and territories ruled by the United Kingdom at its height

concepts.label: Spanish Empire
concepts.definition: Global empire controlled by Spain from the late 15th century to 1976, including territories in the Americas, Europe, Africa and Asia

concepts.label: French Revolution
concepts.definition: Period of radical political and societal change in France from 1789 to 1799 that ended absolute monarchy

concepts.label: Russian Revolution
concepts.definition: Series of revolutions in 1917 that dismantled the Tsarist autocracy and led to the Soviet Union

concepts.label: American Revolution
concepts.definition: Colonial revolt from 1775 to 1783 where thirteen American colonies won independence from British rule

concepts.label: Industrial Revolution
concepts.definition: Period of major technological and socioeconomic change beginning in late 18th century Britain

concepts.label: Renaissance
concepts.definition: Cultural movement spanning roughly from 14th to 17th century beginning in Italy, marking transition from medieval to modern

concepts.label: Reformation
concepts.definition: 16th-century religious movement that challenged the authority of the Catholic Church and led to Protestantism

concepts.label: Enlightenment
concepts.definition: 18th-century intellectual movement emphasizing reason, individualism and skepticism of traditional doctrines

concepts.label: Cold War
concepts.definition: Period of geopolitical tension between the United States and Soviet Union from 1947 to 1991

concepts.label: Holocaust
concepts.definition: Systematic persecution and murder of six million Jews by Nazi Germany during World War II

concepts.label: Great Depression
concepts.definition: Severe worldwide economic downturn lasting from 1929 to late 1930s

concepts.label: Black Death
concepts.definition: Devastating pandemic of bubonic plague that struck Europe and Asia in the mid-14th century

concepts.label: Crusades
concepts.definition: Series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims from 1096 to 1291 for control of the Holy Land

concepts.label: Hundred Years War
concepts.definition: Series of conflicts between England and France from 1337 to 1453 over succession to the French throne

concepts.label: Thirty Years War
concepts.definition: One of the longest and most destructive conflicts in European history, lasting from 1618 to 1648

concepts.label: Seven Years War
concepts.definition: Global conflict from 1756 to 1763 involving most European powers and spanning multiple continents

concepts.label: Napoleonic Wars
concepts.definition: Series of major conflicts from 1803 to 1815 between Napoleon's French Empire and various European coalitions

concepts.label: Crimean War
concepts.definition: Military conflict from 1853 to 1856 where an alliance of France, Britain, Ottoman Empire fought Russia

concepts.label: Franco Prussian War
concepts.definition: Conflict from 1870 to 1871 between Second French Empire and Kingdom of Prussia that led to German unification

concepts.label: Korean War
concepts.definition: Armed conflict from 1950 to 1953 between North and South Korea with major powers supporting each side

concepts.label: Vietnam War
concepts.definition: Conflict from 1955 to 1975 between communist North Vietnam and South Vietnam supported by the United States

concepts.label: Peloponnesian War
concepts.definition: Ancient Greek conflict from 431 to 404 BCE between Delian League led by Athens and Peloponnesian League led by Sparta

concepts.label: Punic Wars
concepts.definition: Series of three wars between Rome and Carthage from 264 to 146 BCE that established Rome as dominant Mediterranean power

concepts.label: Battle Of Hastings
concepts.definition: Decisive Norman victory in 1066 that began Norman conquest of England

concepts.label: Battle Of Waterloo
concepts.definition: Final defeat of Napoleon Bonaparte on June 18, 1815, ending the Napoleonic Wars

concepts.label: Battle Of Gettysburg
concepts.definition: Pivotal battle of American Civil War from July 1-3, 1863, often considered the war's turning point

concepts.label: Battle Of Stalingrad
concepts.definition: Major battle of World War II from August 1942 to February 1943, marking turning point on Eastern Front

concepts.label: D Day
concepts.definition: Allied invasion of Nazi-occupied Western Europe on June 6, 1944, during World War II

concepts.label: United Nations
concepts.definition: International organization founded in 1945 to promote international peace, security, and cooperation

concepts.label: European Union
concepts.definition: Political and economic union of European countries established to promote integration and cooperation

concepts.label: NATO
concepts.definition: North Atlantic Treaty Organization, military alliance formed in 1949 for collective defense

concepts.label: Warsaw Pact
concepts.definition: Military alliance of Eastern European countries led by Soviet Union from 1955 to 1991

concepts.label: League Of Nations
concepts.definition: International organization from 1920 to 1946 designed to maintain world peace after World War I

concepts.label: World Health Organization
concepts.definition: Specialized agency of United Nations responsible for international public health

concepts.label: International Monetary Fund
concepts.definition: International organization working to foster global monetary cooperation and financial stability

concepts.label: World Bank
concepts.definition: International financial institution providing loans and grants to governments for development projects

concepts.label: Congress Of Vienna
concepts.definition: Conference in 1814-1815 that redrew the political map of Europe after Napoleon's defeat

concepts.label: Treaty Of Versailles
concepts.definition: Peace treaty ending World War I between Germany and Allied powers, signed on June 28, 1919

concepts.label: Treaty Of Westphalia
concepts.definition: Series of peace treaties in 1648 ending the Thirty Years War and establishing principle of state sovereignty

concepts.label: Magna Carta
concepts.definition: Charter of rights agreed to by King John of England in 1215, limiting royal power

concepts.label: Declaration Of Independence
concepts.definition: Statement adopted on July 4, 1776, declaring American colonies' independence from British rule

concepts.label: Constitution Of United States
concepts.definition: Supreme law of United States, establishing the framework of government and fundamental rights

concepts.label: Bill Of Rights
concepts.definition: First ten amendments to the US Constitution guaranteeing individual civil liberties

concepts.label: Universal Declaration Of Human Rights
concepts.definition: International document adopted by UN General Assembly in 1948 proclaiming fundamental human rights

concepts.label: Christianity
concepts.definition: Abrahamic monotheistic religion based on the life and teachings of Jesus Christ

concepts.label: Islam
concepts.definition: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded in 7th century CE by Prophet Muhammad

concepts.label: Judaism
concepts.definition: Ethnic religion and one of the oldest monotheistic religions, originating in ancient Israel

concepts.label: Buddhism
concepts.definition: Indian religion or philosophy founded in 5th century BCE by Buddha, emphasizing enlightenment

concepts.label: Hinduism
concepts.definition: Indian religion characterized by belief in reincarnation, karma, and multiple deities

concepts.label: Catholic Church
concepts.definition: Largest Christian church headed by Pope in Vatican City with over 1.3 billion members

concepts.label: Protestant Reformation
concepts.definition: 16th-century movement that challenged Catholic Church authority and created Protestant churches

concepts.label: Orthodox Christianity
concepts.definition: Eastern Christian tradition that separated from Western Christianity in Great Schism of 1054

concepts.label: Sunni Islam
concepts.definition: Largest denomination of Islam, comprising about 85-90% of Muslims worldwide

concepts.label: Shia Islam
concepts.definition: Second-largest denomination of Islam, comprising about 10-15% of Muslims worldwide

concepts.label: Ancient Egypt
concepts.definition: Ancient civilization in northeastern Africa lasting from around 3100 to 30 BCE

concepts.label: Ancient Greece
concepts.definition: Ancient civilization covering period from 800 BCE to 600 CE in Greek peninsula and surrounding regions

concepts.label: Ancient Rome
concepts.definition: Ancient civilization that began as small agricultural community in Italian peninsula around 8th century BCE

concepts.label: Persian Empire
concepts.definition: Series of Iranian empires that ruled over significant portions of ancient world from 6th century BCE

concepts.label: Mesopotamia
concepts.definition: Historical region between Tigris and Euphrates rivers, often called cradle of civilization

concepts.label: Silk Road
concepts.definition: Network of trade routes connecting East and West from 2nd century BCE to 14th century CE

concepts.label: Maya Civilization
concepts.definition: Mesoamerican civilization known for hieroglyphic script, art, architecture, and astronomical systems

concepts.label: Aztec Empire
concepts.definition: Mesoamerican empire centered in Tenochtitlan lasting from 1345 to 1521 CE

concepts.label: Inca Empire
concepts.definition: Largest empire in pre-Columbian America, centered in Andes mountains from 1438 to 1572

concepts.label: Chinese Empire
concepts.definition: Series of imperial dynasties that ruled China from 221 BCE to 1912 CE

concepts.label: Mongol Empire
concepts.definition: Largest contiguous land empire in history, founded by Genghis Khan in 1206

concepts.label: Vikings
concepts.definition: Norse seafarers who raided, traded, and settled throughout Europe from late 8th to 11th centuries

concepts.label: Fall Of Constantinople
concepts.definition: Capture of Byzantine capital by Ottoman Empire on May 29, 1453, ending Byzantine Empire

concepts.label: Age Of Exploration
concepts.definition: Period from 15th to 17th centuries when European explorers discovered new trade routes and lands

concepts.label: Columbus
concepts.definition: Italian explorer whose voyages across Atlantic Ocean initiated widespread European exploration of the Americas

concepts.label: Vasco Da Gama
concepts.definition: Portuguese explorer who was first European to reach India by sailing around Africa

concepts.label: Magellan
concepts.definition: Portuguese explorer whose expedition completed the first circumnavigation of Earth

concepts.label: Colonialism
concepts.definition: Policy or practice of acquiring full or partial political control over another country for economic exploitation

concepts.label: Imperialism
concepts.definition: Policy of extending power and influence through diplomacy or military force over other territories

concepts.label: Feudalism
concepts.definition: Medieval European system where land was held in exchange for service to a lord

concepts.label: Manorialism
concepts.definition: Economic system in medieval Europe where peasants worked land owned by nobles in exchange for protection

concepts.label: Mercantilism
concepts.definition: Economic theory that trade generates wealth and is stimulated by accumulation of profitable balances

concepts.label: Free Market
concepts.definition: Economic system where prices are determined by unrestricted competition between businesses

concepts.label: Planned Economy
concepts.definition: Economic system where production, investment, and allocation are planned by central authority

concepts.label: Mixed Economy
concepts.definition: Economic system combining elements of both capitalist and socialist economic systems

concepts.label: Globalization
concepts.definition: Process of interaction and integration among people, companies, and governments worldwide

concepts.label: Constitutional Monarchy
concepts.definition: Form of monarchy where sovereign exercises authority according to written or unwritten constitution

concepts.label: Absolute Monarchy
concepts.definition: Form of monarchy where sovereign has absolute power and is not restricted by constitution or laws

concepts.label: Parliamentary System
concepts.definition: System of democratic governance where executive derives legitimacy from legislature

concepts.label: Presidential System
concepts.definition: System of government where president serves as head of state and head of government

concepts.label: Federal State
concepts.definition: Political entity characterized by union of partially self-governing provinces under central government

concepts.label: Unitary State
concepts.definition: State where administrative divisions exercise only powers that central government delegates

concepts.label: Confederation
concepts.definition: Union of sovereign groups or states united for purposes of common action

concepts.label: Totalitarianism
concepts.definition: Political system where state holds total authority over society and seeks to control public and private life

concepts.label: Authoritarianism
concepts.definition: Political system characterized by strong central power and limited political freedoms

concepts.label: Liberalism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy based on liberty, consent of governed, and equality before law

concepts.label: Conservatism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy promoting traditional institutions and opposing rapid change

concepts.label: Nationalism
concepts.definition: Political ideology involving strong identification with one's nation and support for its interests

concepts.label: Populism
concepts.definition: Political approach striving to appeal to ordinary people who feel their concerns are disregarded by elites

concepts.label: Anarchism
concepts.definition: Political philosophy advocating for stateless societies based on voluntary institutions

concepts.label: Checks And Balances
concepts.definition: System ensuring no single branch of government becomes too powerful through mutual oversight

concepts.label: Separation Of Powers
concepts.definition: Division of government responsibilities into distinct branches to limit any one branch from exercising core functions

concepts.label: Civil Rights
concepts.definition: Rights of citizens to political and social freedom and equality

concepts.label: Human Rights
concepts.definition: Rights inherent to all human beings regardless of race, sex, nationality, ethnicity, language, religion

concepts.label: Rule Of Law
concepts.definition: Political ideal that all citizens and institutions are accountable to laws that are publicly promulgated

concepts.label: Due Process
concepts.definition: Legal requirement for fair procedures when government deprives person of life, liberty, or property

concepts.label: Habeas Corpus
concepts.definition: Legal principle protecting against unlawful and indefinite imprisonment

concepts.label: Freedom Of Speech
concepts.definition: Right to express opinions and ideas without fear of government retaliation or censorship

concepts.label: Freedom Of Press
concepts.definition: Right to publish and distribute information and opinions without government interference

concepts.label: Freedom Of Religion
concepts.definition: Right to practice any religion or no religion without persecution

concepts.label: Electoral System
concepts.definition: Method by which voters choose between options in election

concepts.label: Majority Rule
concepts.definition: Principle that greater number should exercise greater power in political decisions

concepts.label: Minority Rights
concepts.definition: Individual rights of members of ethnic, religious, or linguistic minorities

concepts.label: Political Party
concepts.definition: Organized group of people with similar political aims and opinions seeking to influence public policy

concepts.label: Interest Group
concepts.definition: Organization of people who share common political interests and work to influence government policy

concepts.label: Lobbyist
concepts.definition: Person who attempts to influence legislation on behalf of special interest group

concepts.label: Civil Society
concepts.definition: Sphere of voluntary associations and institutions that mediate between individuals and state

concepts.label: Public Opinion
concepts.definition: Collective preferences and attitudes of citizens toward matters of government and public policy

concepts.label: Political Culture
concepts.definition: Set of beliefs, values, and norms about politics shared by members of political community

concepts.label: Ideology
concepts.definition: System of ideas and ideals forming basis of economic or political theory

concepts.label: Political Legitimacy
concepts.definition: Right and acceptance of authority to exercise power in political system

concepts.label: Sovereignty
concepts.definition: Authority of state to govern itself or another state

concepts.label: Nation State
concepts.definition: Political unit where state and nation are congruent

concepts.label: Citizenship
concepts.definition: Status of person recognized as legal member of sovereign state

concepts.label: Immigration
concepts.definition: Movement of people into destination country where they are not natives

concepts.label: Refugee
concepts.definition: Person who has been forced to leave their country to escape war, persecution, or natural disaster

concepts.label: Asylum
concepts.definition: Protection granted to someone who has left their home country as political refugee

concepts.label: Diplomacy
concepts.definition: Practice of conducting negotiations between representatives of states or groups

concepts.label: International Law
concepts.definition: Set of rules generally regarded as binding in relations between states and international organizations

concepts.label: Treaty
concepts.definition: Formally concluded and ratified agreement between states under international law

concepts.label: Embassy
concepts.definition: Diplomatic mission representing one country in another country

concepts.label: Ambassador
concepts.definition: Highest ranking diplomatic representative sent by one country to another

concepts.label: Sanctions
concepts.definition: Penalties or restrictive measures imposed by states against other states for political reasons

concepts.label: Alliance
concepts.definition: Union or association formed for mutual benefit between countries or organizations

concepts.label: Multilateralism
concepts.definition: Practice of coordinating relations between three or more states

concepts.label: Unilateralism
concepts.definition: Policy of acting without regard to opinions or interests of other countries

concepts.label: Geopolitics
concepts.definition: Study of effects of geography on international politics and international relations

concepts.label: Balance Of Power
concepts.definition: Theory in international relations that national security is enhanced when military capability is distributed

concepts.label: Realpolitik
concepts.definition: System of politics based on practical rather than moral or ideological considerations

concepts.label: Soft Power
concepts.definition: Ability to influence others through appeal and attraction rather than coercion

concepts.label: Hard Power
concepts.definition: Use of military and economic means to influence behavior or interests of other entities

concepts.label: Hegemony
concepts.definition: Leadership or dominance of one state or social group over others

concepts.label: Superpower
concepts.definition: State with dominant position characterized by extensive ability to exert influence globally

concepts.label: Great Power
concepts.definition: Sovereign state that is recognized as having ability to exert influence on global scale

concepts.label: Middle Power
concepts.definition: Sovereign state that is not superpower but still has significant international influence

concepts.label: Failed State
concepts.definition: Political entity whose government no longer functions effectively

concepts.label: Rogue State
concepts.definition: Nation that threatens peace of other nations and sponsors terrorism

concepts.label: Terrorism
concepts.definition: Use of violence against civilians to achieve political aims

concepts.label: Guerrilla Warfare
concepts.definition: Form of irregular warfare using small groups of combatants to fight larger traditional military

concepts.label: Civil War
concepts.definition: War between organized groups within same state

concepts.label: Revolution
concepts.definition: Fundamental change in political organization occurring over relatively short time

concepts.label: Coup D'etat
concepts.definition: Removal of existing government from power through illegal or unconstitutional means

concepts.label: Martial Law
concepts.definition: Temporary rule by military authorities over civilian population during emergency

concepts.label: State Of Emergency
concepts.definition: Governmental declaration suspending normal constitutional procedures during crisis

concepts.label: Propaganda
concepts.definition: Information used to promote particular political point of view

concepts.label: Censorship
concepts.definition: Suppression of speech, public communication, or information deemed objectionable

concepts.label: Freedom Of Information
concepts.definition: Right to access information held by public bodies

concepts.label: Transparency
concepts.definition: Operating in such way that it is easy for others to see what actions are performed

concepts.label: Accountability
concepts.definition: Answerability and responsibility for actions and decisions

concepts.label: Good Governance
concepts.definition: Manner in which power is exercised in management of economic and social resources

concepts.label: Corruption
concepts.definition: Dishonest or fraudulent conduct by those in power

concepts.label: Bribery
concepts.definition: Offering, giving, receiving or soliciting of value to influence actions of official

concepts.label: Nepotism
concepts.definition: Practice of favoring relatives or friends in political or business matters

concepts.label: Meritocracy
concepts.definition: Governmental system where advancement is based on individual ability or achievement

concepts.label: Bureaucracy
concepts.definition: Administrative system governed by officials in fixed hierarchy

concepts.label: Public Administration
concepts.definition: Implementation of government policy and academic discipline studying this implementation

concepts.label: Civil Service
concepts.definition: Body of government employees working in civil occupations that are neither political nor judicial

concepts.label: Welfare State
concepts.definition: Government undertaking responsibility for welfare of citizens through unemployment insurance and pensions

concepts.label: Social Security
concepts.definition: Government program providing social insurance or protection against economic risks

concepts.label: Universal Healthcare
concepts.definition: Healthcare system providing coverage for all residents of particular country or region

concepts.label: Public Education
concepts.definition: Form of education funded and controlled by local, state, or national government

concepts.label: Social Contract
concepts.definition: Theory that individuals consent to surrender some freedoms to authority for protection of remaining rights

concepts.label: Natural Rights
concepts.definition: Rights that are not contingent upon laws, customs, or beliefs of particular culture

concepts.label: Social Justice
concepts.definition: Concept of fair and just relations between individual and society

concepts.label: Equality
concepts.definition: State of being equal in status, rights, and opportunities

concepts.label: Equity
concepts.definition: Quality of being fair and impartial

concepts.label: Liberty
concepts.definition: State of being free within society from oppressive restrictions

concepts.label: Justice
concepts.definition: Maintenance of what is right according to law, fairness, or equity

concepts.label: Magna Carta Libertatum
concepts.definition: Royal charter of rights agreed to by King John of England in 1215 at Runnymede

concepts.label: English Bill Of Rights
concepts.definition: Act passed by Parliament of England in 1689 that declared rights and liberties of subjects

concepts.label: French Declaration Of Rights
concepts.definition: Human civil rights document from French Revolution defining individual and collective rights

concepts.label: Weimar Republic
concepts.definition: German state from 1918 to 1933, named after city where constitutional assembly took place

concepts.label: Nazi Germany
concepts.definition: German state under control of Adolf Hitler and Nazi Party from 1933 to 1945

concepts.label: Third Reich
concepts.definition: Official Nazi designation for German state from 1933 to 1945

concepts.label: German Democratic Republic
concepts.definition: Socialist state that existed in East Germany from 1949 to 1990

concepts.label: Federal Republic Of Germany
concepts.definition: Democratic federal republic established in 1949 in western Germany

concepts.label: German Reunification
concepts.definition: Political and legal process by which East and West Germany were unified in 1990

concepts.label: Fall Of Berlin Wall
concepts.definition: Event on November 9, 1989 when East German government announced border crossings would be opened

concepts.label: Berlin Wall
concepts.definition: Barrier that divided Berlin from 1961 to 1989, separating East and West Berlin

concepts.label: Iron Curtain
concepts.definition: Political boundary dividing Europe into Soviet and Western spheres of influence during Cold War

concepts.label: Marshall Plan
concepts.definition: American initiative to aid Western Europe economically after World War II

concepts.label: Truman Doctrine
concepts.definition: American foreign policy to counter Soviet expansion during Cold War

concepts.label: Monroe Doctrine
concepts.definition: US policy opposing European colonization of Americas declared in 1823

concepts.label: Cuban Missile Crisis
concepts.definition: 13-day confrontation between US and Soviet Union over Soviet ballistic missiles in Cuba in 1962

concepts.label: Bay Of Pigs Invasion
concepts.definition: Failed landing operation by Cuban exiles supported by US government in 1961

concepts.label: Prague Spring
concepts.definition: Period of political liberalization in Czechoslovakia during 1968

concepts.label: Hungarian Revolution
concepts.definition: Nationwide revolution against Hungarian government and Soviet policies in 1956

concepts.label: Solidarity Movement
concepts.definition: Polish labor union and social movement that played central role in ending communist rule

concepts.label: Velvet Revolution
concepts.definition: Non-violent transition of power in Czechoslovakia from communist to parliamentary republic in 1989

concepts.label: Glasnost
concepts.definition: Soviet policy of open discussion of political and social issues introduced by Gorbachev

concepts.label: Perestroika
concepts.definition: Soviet policy of restructuring political and economic system introduced by Gorbachev

concepts.label: Soviet Union
concepts.definition: Federal socialist state that existed from 1922 to 1991 spanning Eastern Europe and Northern Asia

concepts.label: USSR
concepts.definition: Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, official name of Soviet Union

concepts.label: Bolsheviks
concepts.definition: Faction of Marxist Russian Social Democratic Labour Party led by Vladimir Lenin

concepts.label: Vladimir Lenin
concepts.definition: Russian revolutionary and political theorist who served as first head of government of Soviet Russia

concepts.label: Leon Trotsky
concepts.definition: Russian revolutionary and theorist who was key figure in October Revolution

concepts.label: Nikita Khrushchev
concepts.definition: Leader of Soviet Union from 1953 to 1964 during height of Cold War

concepts.label: Mikhail Gorbachev
concepts.definition: Last leader of Soviet Union who implemented reforms leading to end of Cold War

concepts.label: Chinese Civil War
concepts.definition: Civil war in China fought between Kuomintang and Communist Party from 1927 to 1949

concepts.label: Mao Zedong
concepts.definition: Chinese communist revolutionary who was founding father of People's Republic of China

concepts.label: Great Leap Forward
concepts.definition: Economic campaign by Chinese Communist Party from 1958 to 1962

concepts.label: Cultural Revolution
concepts.definition: Sociopolitical movement in China from 1966 to 1976 initiated by Mao Zedong

concepts.label: Tiananmen Square
concepts.definition: Large city square in center of Beijing known for 1989 pro-democracy protests

concepts.label: Deng Xiaoping
concepts.definition: Chinese politician who led China through economic reforms from 1978 until his retirement

concepts.label: Apartheid
concepts.definition: System of institutionalized racial segregation in South Africa from 1948 to 1994

concepts.label: Nelson Mandela
concepts.definition: South African anti-apartheid revolutionary who became first black president of South Africa

concepts.label: African National Congress
concepts.definition: Political party in South Africa that has governed since end of apartheid

concepts.label: Indian Independence Movement
concepts.definition: Series of activities aimed at ending British rule over Indian subcontinent

concepts.label: Partition Of India
concepts.definition: Division of British India in 1947 into India and Pakistan

concepts.label: Jawaharlal Nehru
concepts.definition: First Prime Minister of India who served from independence in 1947 until his death in 1964

concepts.label: Indira Gandhi
concepts.definition: Prime Minister of India who served from 1966 to 1977 and again from 1980 until assassination in 1984

concepts.label: American Civil War
concepts.definition: Civil war in United States from 1861 to 1865 between Union and Confederacy

concepts.label: Abraham Lincoln
concepts.definition: 16th President of United States who led nation through American Civil War

concepts.label: Emancipation Proclamation
concepts.definition: Presidential proclamation issued by Lincoln in 1863 freeing slaves in rebellious states

concepts.label: Reconstruction Era
concepts.definition: Period in US history from 1865 to 1877 following American Civil War

concepts.label: Jim Crow Laws
concepts.definition: State and local laws enforcing racial segregation in Southern United States

concepts.label: Civil Rights Movement
concepts.definition: Struggle for social justice for African Americans during 1950s and 1960s

concepts.label: Martin Luther King Jr
concepts.definition: American civil rights leader who advanced civil rights through nonviolent civil disobedience

concepts.label: Rosa Parks
concepts.definition: African American activist whose refusal to give up her bus seat sparked Montgomery Bus Boycott

concepts.label: Malcolm X
concepts.definition: African American Muslim minister and human rights activist during civil rights movement

concepts.label: John F Kennedy
concepts.definition: 35th President of United States who served from 1961 until assassination in 1963

concepts.label: Lyndon B Johnson
concepts.definition: 36th President of United States who served from 1963 to 1969

concepts.label: Richard Nixon
concepts.definition: 37th President of United States who served from 1969 to 1974, resigned due to Watergate

concepts.label: Watergate Scandal
concepts.definition: Political scandal in US during 1970s that led to resignation of President Nixon

concepts.label: Ronald Reagan
concepts.definition: 40th President of United States who served from 1981 to 1989

concepts.label: European Economic Community
concepts.definition: Regional organization created by Treaty of Rome in 1957 to integrate European economies

concepts.label: Treaty Of Rome
concepts.definition: International agreement signed in 1957 that created European Economic Community

concepts.label: Maastricht Treaty
concepts.definition: Treaty signed in 1992 that created European Union and established criteria for economic union

concepts.label: Eurozone
concepts.definition: Monetary union of EU member states that have adopted euro as their currency

concepts.label: Schengen Area
concepts.definition: Area comprising European countries that have abolished border controls between them

concepts.label: Brexit
concepts.definition: Withdrawal of United Kingdom from European Union completed in 2020

concepts.label: Margaret Thatcher
concepts.definition: Prime Minister of United Kingdom from 1979 to 1990, known for conservative economic policies

concepts.label: Tony Blair
concepts.definition: Prime Minister of United Kingdom from 1997 to 2007, leader of Labour Party

concepts.label: Charles De Gaulle
concepts.definition: French military leader and statesman who led Free France during World War II

concepts.label: Fifth Republic
concepts.definition: Current republican government of France established in 1958

concepts.label: May 1968
concepts.definition: Period of civil unrest in France characterized by student protests and general strikes

concepts.label: Vichy France
concepts.definition: Client state of Nazi Germany that controlled southern France from 1940 to 1944

concepts.label: Free France
concepts.definition: Political entity claiming to be legitimate government of France during German occupation

concepts.label: Dreyfus Affair
concepts.definition: Political scandal in France from 1894 to 1906 involving accusations of treason

concepts.label: Spanish Civil War
concepts.definition: Civil war in Spain from 1936 to 1939 between Republicans and Nationalists

concepts.label: Francisco Franco
concepts.definition: Spanish general and dictator who ruled Spain from 1939 until his death in 1975

concepts.label: Spanish Transition
concepts.definition: Process by which Spain transitioned from dictatorship to democracy from 1975 to 1982

concepts.label: Benito Mussolini
concepts.definition: Italian political leader who founded fascism and ruled Italy from 1922 to 1943

concepts.label: Italian Unification
concepts.definition: Political and social movement that consolidated Italian states into Kingdom of Italy in 19th century

concepts.label: Giuseppe Garibaldi
concepts.definition: Italian general who played leading role in Italian unification

concepts.label: Risorgimento
concepts.definition: 19th century political and social movement that resulted in consolidation of Italian peninsula

concepts.label: Alexander II
concepts.definition: Emperor of Russia from 1855 to 1881 known for emancipation of serfs

concepts.label: Nicholas II
concepts.definition: Last Emperor of Russia who abdicated in 1917 during Russian Revolution

concepts.label: Russo Japanese War
concepts.definition: War between Russian Empire and Empire of Japan from 1904 to 1905

concepts.label: Treaty Of Portsmouth
concepts.definition: Peace treaty ending Russo-Japanese War signed in Portsmouth, New Hampshire in 1905

concepts.label: Meiji Restoration
concepts.definition: Political revolution in Japan in 1868 that restored imperial rule

concepts.label: Emperor Meiji
concepts.definition: Emperor of Japan from 1867 to 1912 during period of rapid modernization

concepts.label: Tokugawa Shogunate
concepts.definition: Feudal military government of Japan during Edo period from 1603 to 1868

concepts.label: Samurai
concepts.definition: Military nobility and warrior class of medieval and early modern Japan

concepts.label: Pearl Harbor
concepts.definition: Surprise military attack by Imperial Japanese Navy on US naval base on December 7, 1941

concepts.label: Hiroshima
concepts.definition: Japanese city where first atomic bomb was dropped by United States on August 6, 1945

concepts.label: Nagasaki
concepts.definition: Japanese city where second atomic bomb was dropped by United States on August 9, 1945

concepts.label: Manhattan Project
concepts.definition: Research and development program that produced first nuclear weapons during World War II

concepts.label: Nuremberg Trials
concepts.definition: Series of military tribunals held after World War II to prosecute war criminals

concepts.label: United Nations Declaration
concepts.definition: Declaration signed by 26 Allied nations in 1942 during World War II

concepts.label: San Francisco Conference
concepts.definition: Conference held in 1945 that led to creation of United Nations Charter

concepts.label: Security Council
concepts.definition: Principal organ of United Nations charged with maintaining international peace

concepts.label: General Assembly
concepts.definition: Main deliberative organ of United Nations where all member states have equal representation

concepts.label: International Court Of Justice
concepts.definition: Principal judicial organ of United Nations located in The Hague

concepts.label: International Criminal Court
concepts.definition: Intergovernmental organization and international tribunal that prosecutes international crimes

concepts.label: Geneva Conventions
concepts.definition: Four treaties and three protocols that establish standards for humanitarian treatment in war

concepts.label: Hague Conventions
concepts.definition: Two international treaties negotiated at peace conferences in The Hague in 1899 and 1907

concepts.label: Peace Of Westphalia
concepts.definition: Series of peace treaties signed in 1648 ending Thirty Years War

concepts.label: Concert Of Europe
concepts.definition: System of dispute resolution adopted by major European powers after Napoleonic Wars

concepts.label: Balance Of Power Theory
concepts.definition: International relations theory that suggests peace is maintained when military power is distributed

concepts.label: Collective Security
concepts.definition: Security arrangement where each state in system accepts that security of all is concern of all

concepts.label: Mutual Assured Destruction
concepts.definition: Military strategy where full-scale use of nuclear weapons would cause annihilation of both sides

concepts.label: Nuclear Deterrence
concepts.definition: Military strategy under which one state threatens nuclear retaliation if attacked

concepts.label: Arms Race
concepts.definition: Competition between nations to develop superior military capabilities

concepts.label: Space Race
concepts.definition: Competition between United States and Soviet Union to achieve superior spaceflight capability

concepts.label: Sputnik
concepts.definition: First artificial Earth satellite launched by Soviet Union on October 4, 1957

concepts.label: Apollo Program
concepts.definition: US human spaceflight program that achieved goal of landing humans on Moon

concepts.label: Moon Landing
concepts.definition: Apollo 11 mission that first landed humans on Moon on July 20, 1969

concepts.label: Neil Armstrong
concepts.definition: American astronaut who was first person to walk on Moon during Apollo 11 mission

concepts.label: Yuri Gagarin
concepts.definition: Soviet pilot and cosmonaut who was first human to journey into outer space

concepts.label: Cultural Exchange
concepts.definition: Sharing of ideas, values, traditions, and customs between different cultures

concepts.label: Multiculturalism
concepts.definition: Policy promoting coexistence of multiple cultures within single society

concepts.label: Assimilation
concepts.definition: Process by which individuals adopt cultural traits of another group

concepts.label: Integration
concepts.definition: Process of combining or incorporating different groups into unified whole

concepts.label: Segregation
concepts.definition: Separation of groups of people based on characteristics such as race or religion

concepts.label: Discrimination
concepts.definition: Unjust treatment of different categories of people based on characteristics

concepts.label: Prejudice
concepts.definition: Preconceived opinion not based on reason or actual experience

concepts.label: Stereotype
concepts.definition: Oversimplified generalization about particular group or class of people

concepts.label: Social Stratification
concepts.definition: System by which society ranks categories of people in hierarchy

concepts.label: Social Mobility
concepts.definition: Movement of individuals or groups within social hierarchy

concepts.label: Class Struggle
concepts.definition: Tension or conflict between social classes with opposing interests

concepts.label: Proletariat
concepts.definition: Working class in capitalist society who sell their labor for wages

concepts.label: Bourgeoisie
concepts.definition: Capitalist class who own means of production in capitalist society

concepts.label: Karl Marx
concepts.definition: German philosopher and economist who analyzed capitalism and advocated for socialism

concepts.label: Das Kapital
concepts.definition: Critical analysis of capitalism by Karl Marx published in three volumes

concepts.label: Communist Manifesto
concepts.definition: Political pamphlet by Marx and Engels outlining communist theory

concepts.label: Friedrich Engels
concepts.definition: German philosopher who collaborated with Marx on communist theory

concepts.label: John Stuart Mill
concepts.definition: British philosopher who advocated for individual liberty and women's rights

concepts.label: John Locke
concepts.definition: English philosopher known as Father of Liberalism for his theories on government

concepts.label: Thomas Hobbes
concepts.definition: English philosopher known for his work on social contract theory

concepts.label: Jean Jacques Rousseau
concepts.definition: French philosopher who influenced political philosophy and social contract theory

concepts.label: Voltaire
concepts.definition: French philosopher who advocated for civil liberties and freedom of speech

concepts.label: Montesquieu
concepts.definition: French political philosopher who articulated theory of separation of powers

concepts.label: Immanuel Kant
concepts.definition: German philosopher who made significant contributions to ethics and political philosophy

concepts.label: John Rawls
concepts.definition: American political philosopher known for theory of justice and original position

concepts.label: Robert Nozick
concepts.definition: American libertarian philosopher who argued for minimal state

concepts.label: Max Weber
concepts.definition: German sociologist who studied bureaucracy and types of authority

concepts.label: Emile Durkheim
concepts.definition: French sociologist who established academic discipline of sociology

concepts.label: Auguste Comte
concepts.definition: French philosopher who coined term sociology and developed positivism

concepts.label: Social Contract Theory
concepts.definition: Theory that individuals consent to form governments to protect their rights

concepts.label: State Of Nature
concepts.definition: Hypothetical condition before establishment of government or laws

concepts.label: Leviathan
concepts.definition: 1651 book by Thomas Hobbes arguing for strong central authority

concepts.label: Two Treatises Of Government
concepts.definition: Work by John Locke refuting divine right of kings and promoting natural rights

concepts.label: The Social Contract
concepts.definition: 1762 book by Rousseau discussing political legitimacy

concepts.label: Spirit Of Laws
concepts.definition: 1748 work by Montesquieu analyzing different forms of government

concepts.label: Theory Of Justice
concepts.definition: 1971 work by John Rawls on distributive justice and fairness

concepts.label: Anarchy State And Utopia
concepts.definition: 1974 book by Robert Nozick arguing for libertarian minimal state

concepts.label: On Liberty
concepts.definition: 1859 essay by John Stuart Mill on nature and limits of power over individual

concepts.label: Wealth Of Nations
concepts.definition: 1776 book by Adam Smith laying foundations for classical economics

concepts.label: Adam Smith
concepts.definition: Scottish economist and philosopher known as father of modern economics

concepts.label: Invisible Hand
concepts.definition: Economic concept by Adam Smith describing self-regulating market forces

concepts.label: Laissez Faire
concepts.definition: Economic philosophy advocating minimal government intervention in markets

concepts.label: Keynesian Economics
concepts.definition: Economic theory emphasizing government intervention to maintain full employment

concepts.label: John Maynard Keynes
concepts.definition: British economist whose theories revolutionized macroeconomic policy

concepts.label: Milton Friedman
concepts.definition: American economist who advocated for free market capitalism and monetarism

concepts.label: Friedrich Hayek
concepts.definition: Austrian economist and philosopher who defended free market capitalism

concepts.label: Supply And Demand
concepts.definition: Economic model of price determination in market based on relationship between availability and desire

concepts.label: Market Economy
concepts.definition: Economic system where decisions regarding investment and production are guided by price signals

concepts.label: Command Economy
concepts.definition: Economic system where central government makes all decisions about production and distribution

concepts.label: Economic Growth
concepts.definition: Increase in production of economic goods and services over period of time

concepts.label: Gross Domestic Product
concepts.definition: Total monetary value of all finished goods and services produced within country

concepts.label: Inflation
concepts.definition: General increase in prices and fall in purchasing value of money

concepts.label: Unemployment
concepts.definition: Situation when people who are actively seeking work are unable to find jobs

concepts.label: Recession
concepts.definition: Period of temporary economic decline characterized by reduced trade and industrial activity

concepts.label: Depression
concepts.definition: Severe and prolonged downturn in economic activity

concepts.label: Stock Market
concepts.definition: Collection of markets where shares of publicly-held companies are bought and sold

concepts.label: Wall Street Crash
concepts.definition: Stock market crash that occurred in October 1929 marking beginning of Great Depression

concepts.label: Black Tuesday
concepts.definition: October 29, 1929, when stock market crashed losing billions in value

concepts.label: New Deal
concepts.definition: Series of programs by Franklin Roosevelt to address Great Depression

concepts.label: Social Security Act
concepts.definition: 1935 law creating social safety net in United States

concepts.label: World Trade Organization
concepts.definition: International organization dealing with global rules of trade between nations

concepts.label: Free Trade Agreement
concepts.definition: Agreement between countries to eliminate trade barriers

concepts.label: NAFTA
concepts.definition: North American Free Trade Agreement between United States, Canada, and Mexico

concepts.label: European Central Bank
concepts.definition: Central bank for eurozone responsible for monetary policy

concepts.label: Federal Reserve
concepts.definition: Central banking system of United States established in 1913

concepts.label: Bank Of England
concepts.definition: Central bank of United Kingdom established in 1694

concepts.label: Bretton Woods System
concepts.definition: International monetary system established in 1944 based on gold standard

concepts.label: Gold Standard
concepts.definition: Monetary system where standard unit of currency is fixed quantity of gold

concepts.label: Fiat Money
concepts.definition: Currency that government has declared legal tender but not backed by physical commodity

concepts.label: Exchange Rate
concepts.definition: Rate at which one currency will be exchanged for another

concepts.label: Balance Of Trade
concepts.definition: Difference in value of exports and imports of particular country

concepts.label: Trade Deficit
concepts.definition: Economic condition when country imports more than it exports

concepts.label: Trade Surplus
concepts.definition: Economic condition when country exports more than it imports

concepts.label: Tariff
concepts.definition: Tax imposed on imported goods and services

concepts.label: Quota
concepts.definition: Government-imposed trade restriction limiting quantity of good that can be imported

concepts.label: Embargo
concepts.definition: Government order prohibiting trade with particular country

concepts.label: Economic Sanctions
concepts.definition: Commercial and financial penalties applied by one country against targeted country

concepts.label: Poverty
concepts.definition: State of lacking sufficient material possessions or income for basic needs

concepts.label: Income Inequality
concepts.definition: Unequal distribution of income across population

concepts.label: Wealth Gap
concepts.definition: Difference in wealth between richest and poorest members of society

concepts.label: Middle Class
concepts.definition: Social group between upper and working classes typically including professionals and skilled workers

concepts.label: Working Class
concepts.definition: Social group consisting of people employed in manual or industrial work

concepts.label: Upper Class
concepts.definition: Highest social class typically consisting of people with inherited wealth

concepts.label: Caste System
concepts.definition: Social stratification system where social status is inherited and unchangeable

concepts.label: Meritocracy
concepts.definition: Government or holding of power by people selected based on ability

concepts.label: Aristocracy
concepts.definition: Highest class in certain societies holding hereditary titles

concepts.label: Nobility
concepts.definition: Social class possessing more acknowledged privileges than most other classes

concepts.label: Serfdom
concepts.definition: Status of peasants under feudalism bound to hereditary plot of land

concepts.label: Slavery
concepts.definition: System where people are treated as property that can be bought and sold

concepts.label: Abolition Of Slavery
concepts.definition: Movement to end slavery and liberate enslaved people

concepts.label: Underground Railroad
concepts.definition: Network of secret routes used by enslaved people to escape to free states

concepts.label: Harriet Tubman
concepts.definition: African American abolitionist who helped enslaved people escape via Underground Railroad

concepts.label: Frederick Douglass
concepts.definition: African American social reformer and abolitionist who escaped from slavery

concepts.label: Women's Suffrage
concepts.definition: Right of women to vote in political elections

concepts.label: Suffragettes
concepts.definition: Women who advocated for women's right to vote especially in early 20th century

concepts.label: Elizabeth Cady Stanton
concepts.definition: American suffragist and women's rights activist in 19th century

concepts.label: Susan B Anthony
concepts.definition: American suffragist who played pivotal role in women's suffrage movement

concepts.label: Nineteenth Amendment
concepts.definition: Amendment to US Constitution prohibiting denial of voting rights based on sex

concepts.label: Equal Rights Amendment
concepts.definition: Proposed amendment to US Constitution guaranteeing equal legal rights regardless of sex

concepts.label: Women's Liberation Movement
concepts.definition: Political movement seeking equal rights and opportunities for women

concepts.label: Feminism
concepts.definition: Advocacy of women's rights on basis of equality of sexes

concepts.label: Gender Equality
concepts.definition: Equal access to opportunities and treatment regardless of gender

concepts.label: Glass Ceiling
concepts.definition: Invisible barrier preventing advancement of women and minorities in workplace

concepts.label: Pay Gap
concepts.definition: Difference in earnings between different demographic groups

concepts.label: Sexual Harassment
concepts.definition: Unwanted sexual advances or conduct in workplace or other settings

concepts.label: Domestic Violence
concepts.definition: Pattern of abusive behavior used to gain power and control in intimate relationship

concepts.label: Reproductive Rights
concepts.definition: Legal rights relating to reproduction and reproductive health

concepts.label: Roe V Wade
concepts.definition: 1973 US Supreme Court case establishing constitutional right to abortion

concepts.label: Planned Parenthood
concepts.definition: Non-profit organization providing reproductive health care in United States

concepts.label: Birth Control
concepts.definition: Methods used to prevent pregnancy

concepts.label: Family Planning
concepts.definition: Practice of controlling number and spacing of children through contraception

concepts.label: Population Growth
concepts.definition: Increase in number of individuals in population over time

concepts.label: Demographic Transition
concepts.definition: Transition from high birth and death rates to low birth and death rates

concepts.label: Baby Boom
concepts.definition: Period marked by significant increase in birth rate, especially post-WWII

concepts.label: Aging Population
concepts.definition: Demographic phenomenon where median age of population increases

concepts.label: Life Expectancy
concepts.definition: Statistical measure of average lifespan of population

concepts.label: Mortality Rate
concepts.definition: Measure of number of deaths in particular population over specific time period

concepts.label: Birth Rate
concepts.definition: Number of births per thousand people per year

concepts.label: Migration
concepts.definition: Movement of people from one place to another for purpose of settling

concepts.label: Immigration Policy
concepts.definition: Government policies governing movement of people into country

concepts.label: Naturalization
concepts.definition: Legal process by which foreign citizen becomes citizen of new country

concepts.label: Dual Citizenship
concepts.definition: Status of person who is citizen of two countries simultaneously

concepts.label: Stateless Person
concepts.definition: Individual not recognized as national by any state

concepts.label: Human Trafficking
concepts.definition: Trade in humans for purpose of forced labor or commercial sexual exploitation

concepts.label: Genocide
concepts.definition: Deliberate killing of large group of people especially those of particular ethnic group

concepts.label: Crimes Against Humanity
concepts.definition: Deliberate acts committed as part of widespread attack against civilian population

concepts.label: War Crimes
concepts.definition: Violations of laws or customs of war that give rise to individual criminal responsibility

concepts.label: Ethnic Cleansing
concepts.definition: Systematic forced removal of ethnic group from territory

concepts.label: Armenian Genocide
concepts.definition: Systematic extermination of Armenian population by Ottoman Empire during WWI

concepts.label: Rwandan Genocide
concepts.definition: 1994 mass slaughter of Tutsi population in Rwanda by Hutu majority

concepts.label: Bosnian Genocide
concepts.definition: Systematic mass killing of Bosnian Muslims during Bosnian War 1992-1995

concepts.label: Srebrenica Massacre
concepts.definition: July 1995 genocide of Bosnian Muslims in Srebrenica during Bosnian War

concepts.label: Khmer Rouge
concepts.definition: Communist regime in Cambodia from 1975-1979 responsible for Cambodian genocide

concepts.label: Pol Pot
concepts.definition: Cambodian revolutionary who led Khmer Rouge regime responsible for mass killings

concepts.label: Cultural Genocide
concepts.definition: Destruction of cultural heritage and identity of particular group

concepts.label: Truth And Reconciliation Commission
concepts.definition: Body tasked with discovering and revealing past wrongdoing to achieve reconciliation

concepts.label: Transitional Justice
concepts.definition: Set of measures societies use to address legacy of past human rights abuses

concepts.label: Reparations
concepts.definition: Act of making amends for wrong by providing payment or assistance

concepts.label: Restorative Justice
concepts.definition: System of justice focusing on rehabilitation and making amends rather than punishment

concepts.label: International Humanitarian Law
concepts.definition: Branch of international law governing conduct of armed conflict

concepts.label: Just War Theory
concepts.definition: Doctrine of military ethics determining when war is morally justified

concepts.label: Pacifism
concepts.definition: Opposition to war and violence as means of settling disputes

concepts.label: Conscientious Objector
concepts.definition: Person who refuses to bear arms or serve in military on grounds of conscience

concepts.label: Military Draft
concepts.definition: Conscription requiring citizens to serve in armed forces

concepts.label: Volunteer Army
concepts.definition: Military composed entirely of volunteers rather than conscripts

concepts.label: Professional Military
concepts.definition: Armed forces composed of full-time soldiers as career

concepts.label: Citizen Soldier
concepts.definition: Person who serves in military while maintaining civilian occupation

concepts.label: National Guard
concepts.definition: Reserve military force organized by individual states in United States

concepts.label: Veterans Affairs
concepts.definition: Government department providing benefits and services to military veterans

concepts.label: Post Traumatic Stress
concepts.definition: Mental health condition triggered by experiencing or witnessing terrifying event

concepts.label: Shell Shock
concepts.definition: Historical term for combat stress reaction experienced by soldiers

concepts.label: Military Industrial Complex
concepts.definition: Network of relationships between military and defense industry

concepts.label: Defense Spending
concepts.definition: Government expenditure on military forces and related activities

concepts.label: Arms Trade
concepts.definition: Global industry involving sale and transfer of weapons and military technology

concepts.label: Non Proliferation Treaty
concepts.definition: International treaty aimed at preventing spread of nuclear weapons

concepts.label: Disarmament
concepts.definition: Act of reducing, limiting, or abolishing weapons

concepts.label: Arms Control
concepts.definition: International agreements limiting development, production, or deployment of weapons

concepts.label: START Treaty
concepts.definition: Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty between United States and Russia

concepts.label: INF Treaty
concepts.definition: Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty eliminating intermediate range missiles

concepts.label: Test Ban Treaty
concepts.definition: Agreement prohibiting nuclear weapon test explosions

concepts.label: Chemical Weapons Convention
concepts.definition: Treaty prohibiting development, production, and use of chemical weapons

concepts.label: Biological Weapons Convention
concepts.definition: Treaty prohibiting development, production, and stockpiling of biological weapons

concepts.label: Landmine Ban Treaty
concepts.definition: International agreement prohibiting use, production, and transfer of anti-personnel mines

concepts.label: Cluster Munitions Convention
concepts.definition: International treaty prohibiting use and transfer of cluster munitions

concepts.label: Humanitarian Intervention
concepts.definition: Use of military force by state to address humanitarian crisis in another state

concepts.label: Responsibility To Protect
concepts.definition: Political commitment endorsed by UN to prevent genocide and crimes against humanity

concepts.label: Peace Building
concepts.definition: Process of creating sustainable peace in societies emerging from conflict

concepts.label: Conflict Resolution
concepts.definition: Methods of ending disputes between parties through negotiation

concepts.label: Mediation
concepts.definition: Process where neutral third party helps conflicting parties reach agreement

concepts.label: Arbitration
concepts.definition: Resolution of dispute by impartial third party whose decision is binding

concepts.label: Peacekeeping
concepts.definition: Activity intended to preserve peace by preventing or stopping conflicts

concepts.label: Blue Helmets
concepts.definition: United Nations peacekeeping forces identifiable by their blue helmets

concepts.label: Peacekeeping Mission
concepts.definition: Military operation designed to monitor and preserve peace agreements

concepts.label: Peace Enforcement
concepts.definition: Use of military force to compel compliance with peace agreements

concepts.label: Chapter VII
concepts.definition: UN Charter provision allowing Security Council to authorize use of force

concepts.label: Veto Power
concepts.definition: Right of permanent Security Council members to block UN resolutions

concepts.label: Security Council Resolution
concepts.definition: Legal instrument through which UN Security Council exercises its authority

concepts.label: General Assembly Resolution
concepts.definition: Formal expression of opinion by UN General Assembly

concepts.label: International Law Commission
concepts.definition: UN body responsible for codification and progressive development of international law

concepts.label: Customary International Law
concepts.definition: Source of international law derived from consistent state practice

concepts.label: Treaty Law
concepts.definition: Branch of international law governing agreements between states

concepts.label: Jus Cogens
concepts.definition: Fundamental principle of international law accepted by international community

concepts.label: State Immunity
concepts.definition: Principle that sovereign state cannot be sued in foreign court without consent

concepts.label: Diplomatic Immunity
concepts.definition: Legal immunity enjoyed by diplomatic personnel under international law

concepts.label: Vienna Convention
concepts.definition: International treaty governing diplomatic relations between independent countries

concepts.label: Consular Relations
concepts.definition: Official relationship between states through consular offices

concepts.label: Extradition
concepts.definition: Legal process where one jurisdiction delivers person to another jurisdiction

concepts.label: Mutual Legal Assistance
concepts.definition: Cooperation between countries in investigation and prosecution of crimes
